reforms.description: The government has introduced several initiatives to support the startup ecosystem, including the establishment of risk capital funds in both the Walloon and Flanders regions to finance innovative companies and research spin-offs. Additionally, measures to simplify administrative processes for SMEs and the implementation of the Risk Capital Action Plan aim to strengthen the financial infrastructure for commercialization of private research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to enhance the financial support available to startups, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The risk capital funds and administrative simplifications have been put into practice, supporting the startup ecosystem.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of risk capital funds and the implementation of a comprehensive action plan represent significant structural changes in the support for startups.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of a specific policy to encourage SMEs and self-employed persons... the fiscal treatment of SMEs will be modified to stimulate investments by shareholders.

reforms.description: The government has decided to adapt Belgian law to align with European directives on electronic signatures and electronic business, facilitating access to new technologies and promoting high-tech activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By ensuring access to new technologies, this reform is expected to enhance the digital business environment and stimulate innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The decision has been made but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The adaptation of laws to align with EU directives represents a significant but not transformative change in the regulatory framework.
reforms.source_quote: The government has decided to adapt Belgian law to the EC directive on electronic signature, and the forthcoming directive on electronic business will be transposed.

reforms.description: Belgium plans to construct federally owned offshore wind-turbine parks to increase the share of renewable energy in its electricity supply, aiming for a 3% target by 2004, enforced through a system of green certificates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance the renewable energy sector, contributing to sustainable economic growth and energy independence.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been outlined but not yet executed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The construction of offshore wind-turbine parks represents a major investment in renewable energy infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The policy developments in this area are primarily the planned construction of federally owned offshore wind-turbine parks to take advantage of the good wind conditions in coastal waters.
